title: Heartland API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Heartland secret API key detected. Exposing this key allows attackers to authenticate to the Heartland Portico Gateway and perform unauthorized payment transactions, charge arbitrary amounts, issue refunds, void transactions, and access sensitive cardholder data on behalf of the merchant.

Heartland public API key detected. Exposing this key allows attackers to submit fraudulent tokenization requests, enumerate card tokenization endpoints, and potentially abuse the merchant's tokenization quota or conduct targeted attacks using the merchant's integration.
